programme.title: PL STORIES EP46: (Subbed) PHILIPPE COUTINHO
programme.sub-title: We take a look at the career of Philippe Coutinho, tracing his journey from a promising young talent to one of the Premier League’s most skilful and dynamic players. We join Coutinho at his current club Vasco da Gama and reflect on his time in Europe, from his rise at Liverpool to his time at Barcelona, Bayern Munich and Aston Villa. Delving into his passion, creativity, and legacy, we celebrate how his flair and vision have left a lasting mark on the Premier League.

programme.sub-title: We catch up with Juan Mata in Australia, where the former Premier League star is now playing with Western Sydney Wanderers. As he begins a new chapter down under, he also opens up about life after the Premier League and what lies ahead both on and off the pitch.

programme.title: REVIEW OF THE SEASON 2024/2025 (LIV)
programme.sub-title: Relive another extraordinary Premier League campaign as Liverpool soared to an historic 20th league title, Nottingham Forest defied expectations and the promoted sides were strenuously challenged. There were also tactical transformations, shifts in narrative trends, and a more open field than ever before. Review of the Season documents all of the thrilling stories to emerge across the 380 matches.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP05: MANAGER RIVALRIES
programme.sub-title: We peel back the layers on the Premier League’s most iconic managerial rivalries; battles that shaped eras, defined clubs, and captivated fans around the world. From the fierce mind games between Arsène Wenger and Sir Alex Ferguson, to the emotional explosion of Kevin Keegan’s title-chasing Newcastle. From the simmering tension and touchline clashes of Mourinho vs. Wenger, to the tactical chess match between Jürgen Klopp and Pep Guardiola. Through exclusive interviews and unforgettable moments, we explore how these rivalries pushed the limits and left a lasting mark on the Premier League.
programme.desc: We peel back the layers on the Premier League’s most iconic managerial rivalries; battles that shaped eras, defined clubs, and captivated fans around the world. programme.title: REVIEW OF THE SEASON 2024/2025 (LIV)
programme.sub-title: Relive another extraordinary Premier League campaign as Liverpool soared to an historic 20th league title, Nottingham Forest defied expectations and the promoted sides were strenuously challenged. Review of the Season documents all of the thrilling stories to emerge across the 380 matches.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP18: ALAN SHEARER: MY NEWCASTLE STORY
programme.sub-title: A true Premier League icon, Alan Shearer recalls his career at his hometown club Newcastle United. The journey began with a world record transfer in 1996, and culminated with him setting both club and Premier League goalscoring records – achievements that have stood the test of time.
programme.desc: A true Premier League icon, Alan Shearer recalls his career at his hometown club Newcastle United. The journey began with a world record transfer in 1996, and culminated with him setting both club and Premier League goalscoring records – achievements that have stood the test of time.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP20: MCI v QPR 11/12
programme.sub-title: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season. Shaun Wright-Phillips and Joleon Lescott offer the perspective from both sides, with the final day drama at the Etihad set in the context of Manchester City’s Run-In and their pursuit of a first Premier League Title.
programme.desc: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season. Shaun Wright-Phillips and Joleon Lescott offer the perspective from both sides, with the final day drama at the Etihad set in the context of Manchester City’s Run-In and their pursuit of a first Premier League Title.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP06: (Subbed) CLAUDE MAKÉLÉLÉ
programme.sub-title: We sit down with Claude Makélélé to reflect on the career of a true midfield master. Celebrated for redefining the defensive midfield role, Makélélé shares his memories of life in the Premier League, from joining Chelsea during a transformative era to becoming the heartbeat of a title-winning side. With insight from teammates, coaches, and opponents, we explore the intelligence, discipline, and influence that made his name synonymous with the position he perfected.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP35: ROBERT PIRES
programme.sub-title: This week on The Big Interview, two-time Premier League champion, Robert Pires sits down to talk through his career and all things Arsenal. Having joined the club during Arsene Wenger’s reign, Pires played in one of the greatest sides in the clubs’ history - as well as helping them achieve invincible status in 2003/04. The French winger recalls almost quitting football at a young age and how his mother persuaded him to change his mind, winning the World Cup with France, the difficulties he faced when he first joined the Gunners and what this current Arsenal side need to do to win the Premier League title once again.
programme.desc: This week on The Big Interview, two-time Premier League champion, Robert Pires sits down to talk through his career and all things Arsenal. Having joined the club during Arsene Wenger’s reign, Pires played in one of the greatest sides in the clubs’ history - as well as helping them achieve invincible status in 2003/04. The French winger recalls almost quitting football at a young age and how his mother persuaded him to change his mind, winning the World Cup with France, the difficulties he faced when he first joined the Gunners and what this current Arsenal side need to do to win the Premier League title once again.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP35: ROBERT PIRES
programme.sub-title: This week on The Big Interview, two-time Premier League champion, Robert Pires sits down to talk through his career and all things Arsenal. Having joined the club during Arsene Wenger’s reign, Pires played in one of the greatest sides in the clubs’ history - as well as helping them achieve invincible status in 2003/04. The French winger recalls almost quitting football at a young age and how his mother persuaded him to change his mind, winning the World Cup with France, the difficulties he faced when he first joined the Gunners and what this current Arsenal side need to do to win the Premier League title once again.
programme.desc: This week on The Big Interview, two-time Premier League champion, Robert Pires sits down to talk through his career and all things Arsenal. Having joined the club during Arsene Wenger’s reign, Pires played in one of the greatest sides in the clubs’ history - as well as helping them achieve invincible status in 2003/04. The French winger recalls almost quitting football at a young age and how his mother persuaded him to change his mind, winning the World Cup with France, the difficulties he faced when he first joined the Gunners and what this current Arsenal side need to do to win the Premier League title once again.

programme.sub-title: Widely regarded as the two best managers in Premier League history, Sir Alex Ferguson and Arsene Wenger went toe to toe on the touchline on numerous occasions over the years. programme.title: UNDER THE SKIN NOTTINGHAM FOREST
programme.sub-title: This documentary celebrates the incredible, rollercoaster ride of Nottingham Forest Showcasing the club’s fans, culture and traditions, we see how Forest’s return to European football was achieved by reconnecting with the legacy of Brian Clough.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP50: CHRIS WOOD
programme.sub-title: We follow New Zealand striker Chris Wood during a crucial World Cup Qualifier on home soil, at the peak of one of the most impressive seasons of his career. Back in his hometown, we meet the family, coaches and friends who helped shape the player and the person he’s become. From humble beginnings to Premier League mainstay, Wood reflects on the pressures he's faced, the pride of leading his country, and the mindset that's driven him every step of the way.
programme.desc: We follow New Zealand striker Chris Wood during a crucial World Cup Qualifier on home soil, at the peak of one of the most impressive seasons of his career. Back in his hometown, we meet the family, coaches and friends who helped shape the player and the person he’s become. From humble beginnings to Premier League mainstay, Wood reflects on the pressures he's faced, the pride of leading his country, and the mindset that's driven him every step of the way.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP01: (Subbed) JUAN MATA
programme.sub-title: We catch up with Juan Mata in Australia, where the former Premier League star is now playing with Western Sydney Wanderers. The Spaniard reflects on his memorable years in England with Chelsea and Manchester United, sharing insights into his greatest moments, personal growth, and connection with fans. As he begins a new chapter down under, he also opens up about life after the Premier League and what lies ahead both on and off the pitch.

programme.title: Review of the Season 2024/2025 (LIV)
programme.sub-title: Relive another extraordinary Premier League campaign as Liverpool soared to an historic 20th league title, Nottingham Forest defied expectations and the promoted sides were strenuously challenged. There were also tactical transformations, shifts in narrative trends, and a more open field than ever before. Review of the Season documents all of the thrilling stories to emerge across the 380 matches.

There were also tactical transformations, shifts in narrative trends, and a more open field than ever before. programme.title: PL STORIES EP52: CHAMPIONS STORY
programme.sub-title: We dive into the fiercest rivalry in English football—Manchester United and Liverpool. As Liverpool draw level with United’s record haul of top-flight titles, this is a story of history, pride, and the relentless pursuit of greatness. With insight from icons on both sides—Ian Rush, Lou Macari, Brian Kidd, Michael Owen, Phil Thompson, and new Liverpool boss Arne Slot, we unpack the moments that have defined generations, split families, and lit up the Premier League. From legendary goals to seismic title races, this is the story of two giants locked in a battle for supremacy. Woven through it all is the significance of Liverpool’s record-equalling achievement—a milestone that adds a powerful new chapter to football’s greatest rivalry.
programme.desc: We dive into the fiercest rivalry in English football—Manchester United and Liverpool. programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP35: ROBERT PIRES
programme.sub-title: This week on The Big Interview, two-time Premier League champion, Robert Pires sits down to talk through his career and all things Arsenal. Having joined the club during Arsene Wenger’s reign, Pires played in one of the greatest sides in the clubs’ history - as well as helping them achieve invincible status in 2003/04. The French winger recalls almost quitting football at a young age and how his mother persuaded him to change his mind, winning the World Cup with France, the difficulties he faced when he first joined the Gunners and what this current Arsenal side need to do to win the Premier League title once again.
programme.desc: This week on The Big Interview, two-time Premier League champion, Robert Pires sits down to talk through his career and all things Arsenal. Having joined the club during Arsene Wenger’s reign, Pires played in one of the greatest sides in the clubs’ history - as well as helping them achieve invincible status in 2003/04. The French winger recalls almost quitting football at a young age and how his mother persuaded him to change his mind, winning the World Cup with France, the difficulties he faced when he first joined the Gunners and what this current Arsenal side need to do to win the Premier League title once again.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP07: FERGIE v WENGER
programme.sub-title: Widely regarded as the two best managers in Premier League history, Sir Alex Ferguson and Arsene Wenger went toe to toe on the touchline on numerous occasions over the years. But what made their rivalry so fierce? We take a look at the intense clashes, memorable moments on and off the pitch and the monumental impact their duels had on English football's modern era.
programme.desc: Widely regarded as the two best managers in Premier League history, Sir Alex Ferguson and Arsene Wenger went toe to toe on the touchline on numerous occasions over the years. programme.title: PL STORIES EP41: (Subbed) ENZO MARESCA – THE ITALIAN JOB
programme.sub-title: A relationship steeped in glory, Chelsea Football Club is the Italian Job. Last summer, Enzo Maresca became the seventh manager from that heritage to take charge at Stamford Bridge. PL Stories looks back at the success shared between the club and managers from Italy, while also delving into Maresca’s own unique coaching journey as he strives to emulate the achievements of those who came before him.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP06: RODRI & LAMPARD
programme.sub-title: Arguably the best player in his position in world football right now, Rodri has honed his talents on the pitch to help Manchester City achieve a record 4 Premier League titles in a row. In this week’s Big Interview, former Chelsea and City playmaker, Frank Lampard talks to Rodri about the intricacies of his game. From positioning, breaking up play to how he’s developed his role, this midfield masterclass will reveal all about his game.
